receipts.body:
evidence-binding: n2-review 87f991a690b2
verdict: fail
findings_blocking: 1
upstream_read: n1-reap-sweep e725d95472fb

finding: id=R1 scope=in_scope action=fix status=open severity=high fix_role=tdd-guide rationale=sweepBarrierRefs builds its KEEP set from the invoking cwd's repo root but deletes refs from the shared mainRoot ref store — invoked from a linked-worktree cwd it over-reaps LIVE sibling projects (PoC-proven); fix: union the keep universes of root AND mainRoot (both readActiveFolders and the running-set walk, dedupe when equal), propagate x4, add a RED-first worktree-cwd test
finding: id=R2 scope=pre_existing action=document status=deferred severity=low fix_role=none rationale=archive-time reap has no sanitizer-collision guard (sweep does); unreachable for workflow-generated names (issue-N/bundle-* sanitize to identity) and colliding live tags already clobber each other inside the barrier machinery itself
finding: id=R3 scope=out_of_scope action=document status=deferred severity=low fix_role=none rationale=a live folder whose workflow-state.md is unreadable/unparseable drops out of the keep set unless a running-set.json exists; such a project is already invisible to every subsystem (documented pre-existing semantics in claim.js ~635)

## Review: #686 archive-time barrier-ref reap + barrier-ref-sweep

BLOCK on R1 (one open in-scope over-reap of a live project, PoC-proven, the design's named dangerous direction).
Everything else holds: fail-soft scoping, ref-namespace scope (barrier/ only), archive-path coverage,
network-independence, collision under-reap, test mutation resistance, edition parity.

### Attack 1 — FAIL-SOFT past the archive: PASS
The reap (claim.js:2041-2052) sits AFTER the archive block completes and before any return; every git call is
inside the swallow (for-each-ref in the outer try, each update-ref -d in its own inner try/catch). A no-git-repo
archive still returns {archived:true}; mainRoot-resolution failure degrades to reapRoot=root and is caught.

### Attack 2 — NEVER over-reap: FAIL (R1)
Holds: (a) ACTIVE kept, (b) running-set.json kept, (c) sanitizer collision ADDs to keep, (e) network-free
(active-folders.js:253,260 — excludeClosedIssues:false skips prefetchIssueStates/issueIsClosed, no gh call).
BLOCKING: sweepBarrierRefs (claim.js:3119, identical in all ports) builds workflowDir/active from `root`
(getRoot()=cwd toplevel) but for-each-ref/update-ref -d run with cwd=mainRoot (shared ref store). Custom refs
live in the git COMMON dir; a live claim's folder lives in the MAIN root, invisible from a sibling worktree's
kaola-workflow/. PoC (main root live proj-live-b + refs; sweep from a worktree cwd): deleted
refs/kaola-workflow/barrier/proj-live-b/n1 (a LIVE project's gc-anchor). Both keep signals are root-scoped, so
the running-set belt-and-suspenders misses the sibling too. Reachable: the orchestrator's cwd for a run IS the
linked worktree, and a mid-campaign legacy-ref cleanup from a session shell is the anticipated first use. Deleting
the gc-anchor lets git gc prune the live baseline commit, making the barrier_base_mismatch refusal's prescribed
ref-restore repair impossible. Fix: keep set = union of root AND mainRoot universes (readActiveFolders + running-set
walk over both, dedupe when equal); x4; RED-first worktree-cwd test.

### Attack 3 — scope barrier/ only: PASS
Live enumeration confirms only barrier/<tag>/<node> and leg-base/<project>/<node>; barrier-base-* is a .cache
filename, never a ref. Planted refs/kaola-workflow/barrier-x/n1 + leg-base/proj-gone/n1 both SURVIVE (trailing-slash
for-each-ref pattern matches on path boundary). Archive reap deletes only barrier/<that-tag>/*.

### Attack 4 — reap covers every archive path: PASS
All archive paths converge on archiveProjectDir (cmdFinalize 2347, discard 2792, active-folders backstops 3454/3533,
sink-merge 1288). The insertion is after BOTH the linked copy+verify+delete branch and the in-place renameSync
branch. The two early returns (source-missing 1941, archive_incomplete 2010) correctly bypass the reap (safe
direction).

### Attack 5 — mutation-kill the tests: PASS
Snapshot via cp (uncommitted diff), mutate, restore, cmp. Reap-prefix misdirect → 1 RED (reap test). keep.has→false
→ 3 RED (active/collision/running-set KEPT). Restored byte-identical; 209 green. No vacuous tests.

### Attack 6 — edition parity: PASS
edition-sync.js --check green (10 ports, 24 mirrors, 27 byte-identical groups); validate-script-sync green; codex
byte-identical; gitlab/gitea carry the same logic — so R1 must be fixed in all four copies.

findings.raw: id=R1 scope=in_scope action=fix status=open severity=high fix_role=tdd-guide rationale=sweepBarrierRefs builds its KEEP set from the invoking cwd's repo root but deletes refs from the shared mainRoot ref store — invoked from a linked-worktree cwd it over-reaps LIVE sibling projects (PoC-proven); fix: union the keep universes of root AND mainRoot (both readActiveFolders and the running-set walk, dedupe when equal), propagate x4, add a RED-first worktree-cwd test

id=R2 scope=pre_existing action=document status=deferred severity=low fix_role=none rationale=archive-time reap has no sanitizer-collision guard (sweep does); unreachable for workflow-generated names (issue-N/bundle-* sanitize to identity) and colliding live tags already clobber each other inside the barrier machinery itself

id=R3 scope=out_of_scope action=document status=deferred severity=low fix_role=none rationale=a live folder whose workflow-state.md is unreadable/unparseable drops out of the keep set unless a running-set.json exists; such a project is already invisible to every subsystem (documented pre-existing semantics in claim.js ~635)

evidence-binding: n2-review 41fd131b08b7
verdict: pass
findings_blocking: 0
upstream_read: n1-reap-sweep e725d95472fb

finding: id=R1 scope=in_scope action=fix status=resolved severity=high fix_role=none rationale=union-of-root-and-mainRoot keep-scan closes the worktree-cwd over-reap; PoC re-executed kept the live sibling AND still reaped the true orphan (discrimination intact); mutation-kill (revert union to [root]) reproduced exactly the 2 R1 assertions RED; restore cmp byte-identical; the union only ADDS to KEEP so it is structurally incapable of a new deletion; readActiveFolders accepts mainRoot cleanly + network-free; dedupe correct; fail-soft geometry preserved
finding: id=R2 scope=pre_existing action=document status=deferred severity=low fix_role=none rationale=archive-time reap has no sanitizer-collision guard; unreachable for workflow-generated names
finding: id=R3 scope=out_of_scope action=document status=deferred severity=low fix_role=none rationale=a live folder with unreadable workflow-state drops from keep unless running-set.json exists; pre-existing invisible-to-every-subsystem semantics

# Re-review (attempt 2) — #686 n1-reap-sweep repair

## V1 — R1 closed (executed independent PoC)
Fresh main repo + linked worktree; live sibling `issue-sib` (workflow-state in_progress + running-set.json) ONLY
under main root; plus a true orphan `issue-orphan` (no folder). Sweep with cwd=worktree →
`{"refsDeleted":["refs/kaola-workflow/barrier/issue-orphan/n1"],"tagsKept":["issue-sib"],"tagsDeleted":["issue-orphan"]}`
— live sibling survived AND the true orphan still reaped (not a blanket keep-all). PASS.

## V2 — mutation-kill the regression
Revert ONLY the union (`scanRoots` → `[root]`) → 2 R1 assertions RED (`tagsDeleted=["issue-686sibling"]`),
211 passed — matches the pre-fix defect. Restore cmp byte-identical → 213 green. Test binds to the union. PASS.

## V3 — no new over/under-reap from the union
Structural: union only ADDS to KEEP; deletion set = enumerated − KEEP, so union can only SHRINK deletions —
cannot introduce a new over-reap. Under-reap: a mainRoot-scanned keep = a genuinely live main-root project (the
fix). Live claims always have a main-root folder (verified against the live issue-686 run). Orphans still reaped
from a worktree cwd and when main root has no kaola-workflow/ dir (executed: no throw, orphan deleted).
readActiveFolders(scanRoot,{excludeClosedIssues:false}) accepts an explicit root (active-folders.js:230) and skips
the gh prefetch — network-free (ran without OFFLINE: exit 0, idempotent). Dedupe mainRoot===root → single walk;
a Set makes any path mismatch harmless. Fail-soft: keep-set built before all deletions in one try; a scan fault
aborts before any update-ref -d → under-reap, never over-reap. PASS.

## V4 — prior guarantees intact
All earlier #686 blocks green in the 213 run (reap+archive-success, fail-soft no-git-repo, active keep,
archived-no-folder delete, collision keep, running-set keep, leg-base scope guard). Test diff append-only;
archiveProjectDir reap region unchanged. Walkthrough exit 0. PASS.

## V5 — edition parity
edition-sync.js --check green (10 ports, 24 mirrors, 27 byte-identical groups); validate-script-sync green; the
scanRoots union line present once in each of the 4 copies; canonical == codex cmp byte-identical. PASS.

APPROVE — R1 genuinely closed (executed PoC), regression mutation-verified, union structurally safe, all prior
guarantees + parity hold. (Loose comment prose at claim.js:3103 "ONE root only" vs the live both-presence is
cosmetic; keep-if-seen-in-EITHER makes both-presence strictly safe — below reporting threshold, not filed.)

evidence-binding: n3-adversary 4877b0b8fa5a
verdict: fail
findings_blocking: 2
upstream_read: n1-reap-sweep e725d95472fb

finding: id=R4 scope=in_scope action=fix status=open severity=high fix_role=tdd-guide rationale=keep-set universe {invoking root, mainRoot} is smaller than the reachable claim-root universe {ANY linked worktree root}; executed end-to-end with only shipped commands — claim from worktree w1 cwd + real record-base + sweep from main root deleted a LIVE claim's anchor, then git gc pruned the baseline commit (barrier_base_mismatch ref-restore now impossible); the R1 union closed only half the class (R1: sweep-from-worktree misses main-root claim; R4: sweep-from-main/other-worktree misses worktree claim); FIX: build both keep passes over ALL `git worktree list --porcelain` roots (legs under .kw/legs are worktrees too), or fail-closed abort when unscanned worktrees exist
finding: id=R5 scope=in_scope action=fix status=open severity=low fix_role=tdd-guide rationale=case-insensitive-FS tag/dirent mismatch — a wrong-case --record-base path anchors tag `Issue-9` while the live folder dirent is `issue-9`; sweep reaped the live anchor (executed on macOS case-insensitive FS); FIX: case-insensitive keep comparison (only ADDS to KEEP → fail-safe under-reap on every FS)
finding: id=R6 scope=pre_existing action=document status=deferred severity=low fix_role=none rationale=confirms R2 by execution via REAL archiveProjectDir — archiving live-claimed `fix-v1.2` reaped LIVE `fix-v1_2` anchor; correction to R2: reachable via user-supplied --project names (isSafeName accepts dots/spaces/*), not only workflow-generated names; collision blast radius pre-exists at record time (shared projTag namespace); concur deferred
finding: id=R7 scope=in_scope action=document status=deferred severity=low fix_role=none rationale=TOCTOU window (keep built claim.js:3143-3160 strictly before enumeration :3166, no lock in cmdBarrierRefSweep) — a claim+record-base landing inside the ms-scale window is reaped; realistic damage case subsumed by R4; document sweep-at-quiet-window posture

# Adversarial gate — #686 reap/sweep (attempt 1)

## Attack 1 — third-universe over-reap (EXECUTED, BROKE THE CLAIM)
Main repo + linked worktrees w1, w2. Live `issue-w2live` (active state + running-set.json) ONLY in w2; ref
recorded from w2. Sweep from w1: refsDeleted includes `refs/kaola-workflow/barrier/issue-w2live/n1`,
tagsDeleted:["issue-dead","issue-w2live"] — LIVE sibling-worktree project over-reaped. scanRoots=[root,mainRoot]
covers two universes; a live project in any OTHER worktree is invisible.

## Attack 1b — reachability via supported commands only (EXECUTED, catastrophic end-state)
1. `claim --project issue-777` cwd=worktree w1 → acquired; folder ONLY at w1/kaola-workflow/issue-777/, main root
   has no kaola-workflow/ (no lane/cwd fence — cmdClaim/cmdStartup use getRoot()=cwd toplevel).
2. real `plan-validator --record-base --node-id n1` → refs/kaola-workflow/barrier/issue-777/n1 anchored.
3. `barrier-ref-sweep` from MAIN root → deletes issue-777 anchor (root==mainRoot dedupes to one universe; w1 invisible).
4. `git gc --prune=now` → baseline commit GONE while w1/.cache/barrier-base-n1 still names it → ref-restore repair impossible.
Falsifies n2's "live claims always have a main-root folder" (happy-path observation, not enforced). R4 is the mirror of R1.

## Attack 2 — case-insensitive-FS tag/dirent mismatch (EXECUTED, broke it)
macOS case-insensitive FS. Live folder `issue-9`; real --record-base with wrong-case path `.../Issue-9/...` anchors
tag `Issue-9` (projTag = basename AS GIVEN, plan-validator.js:2912). Sweep reaped `barrier/Issue-9/n1` (live).
Off-nominal precondition → low; fix strictly fail-safe (case-insensitive keep only ADDs).

## Attack 3 — archive-reap prefix boundary (EXECUTED, HELD)
Live issue-68 + dying issue-6. Real archiveProjectDir(root,'issue-6') reaped only barrier/issue-6/n1;
barrier/issue-68/n1 SURVIVED. Trailing-slash for-each-ref matches on a path boundary — verified.

## Attack 4 — sanitizer collision via user name (EXECUTED, confirms R2/R6)
CLI accepts `--project fix-v1.2` (isSafeName rejects only / \ NUL . ..). record-base for live fix-v1_2 anchored
barrier/fix-v1_2/n1; archiveProjectDir('fix-v1.2') reaped it. Reachable via user names; blast radius pre-exists at
record time. Deferred (R6).

## Attack 5 — hostile refnames / fail-open (EXECUTED, HELD)
git update-ref REFUSES `a b`/`a*b`/`a[b`/`a..b` tags; a malformed packed-refs entry is IGNORED by for-each-ref;
sweep with it present deleted nothing wrong. Deletion uses exact refnames (no globbing); archive-reap pattern built
from the [A-Za-z0-9_-]-restricted sanitized tag — no glob metachar can enter.

## Attack 6 — concurrency (code-read, window subsumed)
No lock in cmdBarrierRefSweep; keep built before enumeration → ms-scale window (R7, subsumed by R4). Archive reap is
tag-scoped to the dying project, fires after the live copy is gone — no cross-project hole (except R2/R6 collision).

## Verdict
REFUTED (high). Two executed over-reaps: R4 (supported-commands third-universe → live anchor deleted → baseline
gc-pruned, repair impossible) + R5 (case-insensitive tag/dirent). R4's fix (all-worktree-roots keep scan,
fail-closed on enumeration failure) closes R4+A1; a case-folded keep closes R5. No repo file modified.

findings.raw: id=R4 scope=in_scope action=fix status=open severity=high fix_role=tdd-guide rationale=keep-set universe {invoking root, mainRoot} is smaller than the reachable claim-root universe {ANY linked worktree root}; executed end-to-end with only shipped commands — claim from worktree w1 cwd + real record-base + sweep from main root deleted a LIVE claim's anchor, then git gc pruned the baseline commit (barrier_base_mismatch ref-restore now impossible); the R1 union closed only half the class (R1: sweep-from-worktree misses main-root claim; R4: sweep-from-main/other-worktree misses worktree claim); FIX: build both keep passes over ALL `git worktree list --porcelain` roots (legs under .kw/legs are worktrees too), or fail-closed abort when unscanned worktrees exist

id=R5 scope=in_scope action=fix status=open severity=low fix_role=tdd-guide rationale=case-insensitive-FS tag/dirent mismatch — a wrong-case --record-base path anchors tag `Issue-9` while the live folder dirent is `issue-9`; sweep reaped the live anchor (executed on macOS case-insensitive FS); FIX: case-insensitive keep comparison (only ADDS to KEEP → fail-safe under-reap on every FS)

id=R6 scope=pre_existing action=document status=deferred severity=low fix_role=none rationale=confirms R2 by execution via REAL archiveProjectDir — archiving live-claimed `fix-v1.2` reaped LIVE `fix-v1_2` anchor; correction to R2: reachable via user-supplied --project names (isSafeName accepts dots/spaces/*), not only workflow-generated names; collision blast radius pre-exists at record time (shared projTag namespace); concur deferred

id=R7 scope=in_scope action=document status=deferred severity=low fix_role=none rationale=TOCTOU window (keep built claim.js:3143-3160 strictly before enumeration :3166, no lock in cmdBarrierRefSweep) — a claim+record-base landing inside the ms-scale window is reaped; realistic damage case subsumed by R4; document sweep-at-quiet-window posture

id=R6 scope=pre_existing action=document status=deferred severity=low fix_role=none rationale=confirms R2 by execution via REAL archiveProjectDir — archiving live-claimed `fix-v1.2` reaped LIVE `fix-v1_2` anchor; correction to R2: reachable via user-supplied --project names (isSafeName accepts dots/spaces/*), not only workflow-generated names; collision blast radius pre-exists at record time (shared projTag namespace); concur deferred

id=R7 scope=in_scope action=document status=deferred severity=low fix_role=none rationale=TOCTOU window (keep built claim.js:3143-3160 strictly before enumeration :3166, no lock in cmdBarrierRefSweep) — a claim+record-base landing inside the ms-scale window is reaped; realistic damage case subsumed by R4; document sweep-at-quiet-window posture

receipts.body:
evidence-binding: n2-review 875d189fc5cc
verdict: pass
findings_blocking: 0
upstream_read: n1-reap-sweep e725d95472fb

finding: id=R4 scope=in_scope action=fix status=resolved severity=high fix_role=none rationale=third-worktree over-reap closed by full git-worktree-list scan union; PoC re-executed KEPT the third-worktree AND leg-worktree claims, dead tag still reaped, mutation M1 (revert scanRoots) killed by the targeted test
finding: id=R5 scope=in_scope action=fix status=resolved severity=low fix_role=none rationale=case-insensitive-FS tag/dirent mismatch closed by add-only case-folded keep membership (keepLower); PoC re-executed KEPT with real-case reporting, mutation M2 killed
finding: id=R6 scope=in_scope action=document status=resolved severity=low fix_role=none rationale=enum-failure fail-closed abort verified (deletes nothing, recovers next run, never fires spuriously on healthy/prunable-worktree repos); mutation M3 killed
finding: id=R7 scope=out_of_scope action=none status=deferred severity=low fix_role=none rationale=stale worktree with surviving dir + abandoned folder keeps its tag until worktree cleanup runs — safe under-reap by design; existing cleanup paths converge it

# n2-review re-review pass 3 — R4+R5 delta

## R4 closed (executed PoC)
main + 3 linked worktrees (w1 invoker, w2 with the ONLY copy of live issue-w2live, a .kw/legs leg with the ONLY
copy of issue-leglive) + dead tag issue-dead. Sweep cwd=w1 → tagsKept=["issue-leglive","issue-w2live"] (both
third/leg-worktree claims survive), tagsDeleted=["issue-dead"] (discrimination retained). scanRoots =
union(git worktree list, root, mainRoot) (claim.js:3182) is a strict superset of R1 → keep only grows, deletions
only shrink; no new over-reap surface.

## Fail-closed on enum failure (executed)
KAOLA_WORKFLOW_FORCE_BARRIER_WT_LIST_FAIL=1 → {"aborted":true,"reason":"worktree_enum_failed","refsDeleted":[]},
dead ref survived; abort at claim.js:3175-3179 BEFORE any keep-build/enumeration. Env unset next run → normal
reap. No spurious abort on healthy / multi-worktree / prunable-stale-worktree repos.

## R5 closed (executed)
folder issue-9 (lowercase dirent) + ref tag Issue-9 (wrong case) + genuinely-dead ISSUE-DEAD9 → tagsKept=["Issue-9"],
tagsDeleted=["ISSUE-DEAD9"]. keepLower (claim.js:3202) built alongside keep, never replacing → add-only; ASCII-only
tag alphabet rules out Unicode edge cases.

## Mutation kills (all killed, cmp byte-identical restore)
M1 scanRoots→[root,mainRoot]: R4 test RED (223 passed). M2 keepLower→exact: R5 test RED (223). M3 fail-closed
abort→proceed: enum-fail test RED (222). Post-restore 225 green. Precisely scoped, not vacuous.

## Wider-scan posture
Over-reap impossible (superset → keep grows). Under-reap: a prunable worktree whose DIR is deleted contributes
nothing (readActiveFolders []) → its tags correctly reaped; a stale worktree with a surviving abandoned folder
keeps its tag until cleanup (safe, R7). aborted:true summary shape breaks no consumer (only the 4 claim.js + test
consume the JSON).

## Parity (executed)
edition-sync.js --check green; validate-script-sync green; all 4 copies carry listBarrierSweepWorktreeRoots +
keepLower + worktree_enum_failed; canonical==codex cmp byte-identical; walkthrough passed; four-chain recorded in
n1 evidence.

APPROVE — R4/R5 closed with executed proof, 3 mutations killed, fail-closed real, wider scan monotonically safer,
base+R1 not regressed (225/225), parity byte-verified ×4. Change gate passes.

id=R5 scope=in_scope action=fix status=resolved severity=low fix_role=none rationale=case-insensitive-FS tag/dirent mismatch closed by add-only case-folded keep membership (keepLower); PoC re-executed KEPT with real-case reporting, mutation M2 killed

id=R6 scope=in_scope action=document status=resolved severity=low fix_role=none rationale=enum-failure fail-closed abort verified (deletes nothing, recovers next run, never fires spuriously on healthy/prunable-worktree repos); mutation M3 killed

id=R7 scope=out_of_scope action=none status=deferred severity=low fix_role=none rationale=stale worktree with surviving dir + abandoned folder keeps its tag until worktree cleanup runs — safe under-reap by design; existing cleanup paths converge it

evidence-binding: n3-adversary 559efaf0bdc4
verdict: fail
findings_blocking: 2
upstream_read: n1-reap-sweep e725d95472fb

finding: id=R6 scope=in_scope action=fix status=open severity=medium fix_role=tdd-guide rationale=porcelain LF-split parse corrupts an embedded-newline worktree path — a `git worktree add` path containing a literal \n emits the path RAW across two physical lines; the split('\n')+indexOf('worktree ') parse (claim.js:3122-3124) captures a nonexistent prefix and drops the remainder, so a live claim in that worktree (both keep signals) is silently over-reaped; breaches the fix's own "unscannable ⇒ delete nothing" invariant (mis-parseable output, yet deletion proceeded); FIX: parse `git worktree list --porcelain -z` byte-exact (NUL-separated; supported git 2.36+, confirmed on this 2.54.0)
finding: id=R7 scope=in_scope action=fix status=open severity=medium fix_role=tdd-guide rationale=.trim() strips trailing whitespace that is PART of the worktree path (claim.js:3124) — a trailing-space worktree path resolves to a nonexistent root → live claim invisible → over-reaped; distinct flaw from R6 (the trim, not the LF split), SAME fix locus; the `--porcelain -z` byte-exact parse closes both (do NOT .trim() a -z field)
finding: id=R8 scope=out_of_scope action=document status=deferred severity=low fix_role=none rationale=a worktree moved by plain `mv` without `git worktree repair` stays git-functional but `worktree list` reports the stale old path → over-reap; git's OWN registry (list/prune) is stale the identical way, so this is inherited git bookkeeping degradation, not a union defect; document the "run git worktree repair after moving a worktree" limitation in the ref-lifecycle docs (n4)

# Adversarial re-attack (attempt 2) — #686 reap/sweep after R4+R5

## T0/T2 — real adaptive geometry (surface 2): SURVIVED
This run's geometry: `git worktree list --porcelain` = {main root, .kw/worktrees/issue-686}; kaola-workflow/issue-686/
active in BOTH; refs in the shared common dir. No-delete replica from the issue-686 worktree root: wouldKeep=["issue-686"],
wouldDelete = 43 tags (all archived bundles/issues bundle-617-618 … issue-682). The sweep cannot reap this run's own
live anchors; legacy-reclaim purpose confirmed live. Also: main-root-only claim, third worktree, .kw/legs leg all kept.

## T1b — embedded NEWLINE worktree path (surface 1): REFUTES (R6)
`git worktree add` accepts a path with a literal \n (APFS); `--porcelain` (no -z) emits it RAW across two lines
(cat -ve verified). The split('\n')+indexOf parse captures nonexistent prefix `…/t1/nl`, drops the remainder → a
live claim (status:active + running-set.json, BOTH signals) over-reaped: refsDeleted=["refs/kaola-workflow/barrier/issue-30/n1"].

## T1c — TRAILING SPACE worktree path (surface 1): REFUTES (R7)
`git worktree add "…/wt-trail "` accepted; porcelain emits `worktree …/wt-trail ` (trailing space part of path,
cat -ve verified); the .trim() strips it → nonexistent root → live claim invisible → refsDeleted=["…/issue-40/n1"].
Distinct flaw from R6; same fix locus. Repair for both: `--porcelain -z` byte-exact NUL-separated parse (probe
confirmed supported on git 2.54.0; available 2.36+).

Weighting (honest): the workflow's own layout (.kw/worktrees/<project>, .kw/legs, isSafeName names) never produces
such paths — only a user-placed clone/worktree with an embedded-newline or trailing-whitespace filesystem path.
Likelihood very low; impact is the dangerous irreversible gc-anchor deletion (R4 severity class); both EXECUTED.

## T6 — worktree moved by mv without git worktree repair (surfaces 1/5): over-reap, OUT-OF-SCOPE (R8)
Git functional inside the moved worktree but `worktree list` reports the stale old path → live claim invisible →
refs deleted. Git's OWN bookkeeping is stale identically (git documents `worktree repair` as mandatory after manual
moves). Document, don't fix — the "parsed-root-must-exist" backstop would incidentally fence it but would spuriously
abort on benign prunable-but-unpruned worktrees.

## Surfaces that SURVIVED
T3a maxBuffer: execFileSync THROWS ENOBUFS on overflow (never returns truncated) → catch → fail-closed abort.
T3b locked/prunable worktrees: locked still enumerated+kept; prunable (dir deleted) lists but scans empty (claim
folder gone with the dir — nothing live). T3c forced enum fail: aborted:true, refsDeleted:[]. T7/T7b odd roots:
non-repo → throw → abort; .git dir → resolveMainRoot normalizes. T4 case-fold: sanitizeBarrierTag ASCII-only
[A-Za-z0-9_-] (Turkish İ / Kelvin K / ß / é / CJK probed) → locale-independent toLowerCase exact; foreign tags fold
toward keep (under-reap). T5 symlink/trailing-slash/dedup: git records canonical realpath; Set dedup harmless.

## Suite + parity: 225 assertions pass; 3 plugin editions byte-identical except forge comment rename-normalization.

## Verdict
REFUTED (high). Two executed in-scope over-reaps of live gc-anchors (R6 newline, R7 trailing-space) at
claim.js:3122-3124, one fix locus (`--porcelain -z` byte-exact, no .trim). All other surfaces survived. No repo
file modified; issue-686's 3 live anchors intact.

finding: id=R6 scope=in_scope action=fix status=resolved severity=medium fix_role=none rationale=CLOSED — re-executed in a fresh scratch repo: real `git worktree add` embedded-LF path, live claim rooted only there, sweep from main root → tagsKept=["issue-r6live"], live ref intact, co-resident dead tag reaped; the `--porcelain -z` NUL-split parse (claim.js:3133-3150, no .trim) round-trips the path byte-exact; suite test mutation-killed (revert to plain --porcelain → R6 assertion RED)

finding: id=R9 scope=in_scope action=fix status=deferred filed=#690 severity=low fix_role=tdd-guide rationale=R6 test fixture leaks its embedded-newline worktree dir into os.tmpdir (finally cleans only mainRoot686g, not the sibling nlPath686g); test-only, no product impact, R6/R7 assertions unaffected; filed #690 with the exact one-line finally cleanup + hoist ready to apply — deferred (a 4th n1 repair cycle is disproportionate for a low test-hygiene leak per axiom 3)

# n2-review (attempt 3) — R6+R7 fix (`--porcelain -z` parse)

## R6 closed (executed)
Fresh scratch repo: real `git worktree add` at an embedded-newline path; live claim (in_progress + running-set.json)
rooted ONLY there; sweep from main root → {"refsDeleted":["…/issue-dead/n1"],"tagsKept":["issue-r6live"],
"tagsDeleted":["issue-dead"]} — live ref survived AND dead tag reaped same pass.

## R7 closed (executed)
`git worktree add "…/wt-trail "` (trailing space), live claim rooted only there → tagsKept=["issue-r7live"], ref
intact, dead tag reaped.

## -z parse correctness (read + executed)
listBarrierSweepWorktreeRoots (claim.js:3133-3150): `git worktree list --porcelain -z`, split on \0, keep fields
with exact prefix `worktree `, take slice('worktree '.length) with NO .trim(). Record-boundary empty fields + non-
worktree attribute fields (HEAD/branch/prunable) fail the prefix test → skipped; no spurious empty root. Happy path
executed: 4-root repo, sweep from a linked worktree → all 4 tags kept (every root enumerated across record
boundaries), dead tag reaped.

## No nonexistent-root abort (executed, decision held)
Prunable worktree (dir rm -rf'd, not pruned): still listed with a prunable attr; sweep from main → no abort, prunable
root contributed nothing, live main-root claim kept, dead tag reaped. The deliberate omission of a "root must exist ⇒
abort" backstop held (correct: under -z a nonexistent parsed root genuinely means a prunable worktree; aborting would
spuriously block safe reaping).

## Mutation-kill (executed)
Snapshot to scratchpad, revert ONLY the parse (drop -z, restore split('\n')/.trim()): 4 failures (the R6/R7
assertions, refsDeleted showing the real over-reap), 231 passed. Restore cmp byte-identical → 235 green. Load-bearing.

## No regression (executed)
235 assertions green (225 prior + 10 new). Forced-enum-THROW fail-closed re-executed: aborted:true, dead tag intact.
Walkthrough passed.

## Edition parity (executed)
edition-sync.js --check green; validate-script-sync green; all 4 copies carry the identical `'--porcelain','-z'` +
trim-free NUL-split loop. Two pre-existing plain --porcelain call sites (worktreeRegistered :595, listWorkflowWorktrees
:2894) are out of scope and non-destructive (idempotence probe / read-only listing).

PASS — R6/R7 closed (executed, mutation-killed, parity ×4), happy-path + fail-closed + prunable postures intact, no
regression. R9 (test-hygiene tmpdir leak) deferred + filed #690 (exact fix ready); R8 routed to docs.

evidence-binding: n3-adversary a3c5bdadf39c
verdict: fail
findings_blocking: 1
upstream_read: n1-reap-sweep e725d95472fb

finding: id=R8 scope=in_scope action=fix status=open severity=medium fix_role=tdd-guide rationale=per-FOLDER unreadable workflow-state.md (EACCES chmod-000 or EISDIR) is swallowed inside readActiveFolders (active-folders.js:246 catch→continue), so a live project with no running-set.json (a SEQUENCE run — the common case, confirmed this very run has no running-set.json) drops its SOLE keep signal per-folder and the sweep reaps its live barrier gc-anchor; contradicts the sweep's OWN documented "cannot prove dead ⇒ keep/abort" posture (claim.js:3182-3188) which currently binds only at DIRECTORY granularity (chmod-000 on kaola-workflow/ aborts — repoD executed {aborted:true}, both refs survived — but chmod-000 on ONE state file silently drops that folder); EXECUTED: repoA reaped barrier/issue-13/n1 (chmod 000 state file), repoE reaped barrier/issue-50/n1 (state file is a DIRECTORY / EISDIR); FIX tighten-only + sweep-local: in sweepBarrierRefs treat a folder whose workflow-state.md EXISTS but cannot be read as KEEP (unprovable-dead ⇒ keep); do NOT alter shared readActiveFolders semantics (other #353 consumers are read-only). Reachability requires out-of-band FS damage (no shipped flow makes an unreadable state file — writeState is atomic-replace), hence not high, but the impact is the dangerous irreversible direction and it is strictly less exotic than the accepted R6/R7.

finding: id=R9 scope=in_scope action=fix status=deferred filed=#690 severity=low fix_role=tdd-guide rationale=R6 fixture tmpdir leak (n2 finding), filed with the exact fix ready

# Adversarial gate — #686 reap/sweep (attempt 3, post R6/R7)

## Surface 1 — keep SIGNAL (REFUTATION, R8)
12-state matrix (repoA/repoE), each a live ref. KEPT correctly: in_progress, unknown-status, claimed, bundle,
running-set-only, empty-state-file(→unknown). REAPED correctly: status:released (cmdRelease archives via
archiveProjectDir → legacy residue), status:closed (mid-archive stamped window, terminal), folder-no-state-no-rs
(no liveness evidence; state file precedes any ref by construction), dot-dir + symlinked-dir (invisible to the
ENTIRE workflow, can never be claimed → never acquire refs). REAPED WRONGLY (R8): issue-13 state file chmod 000
(EACCES) → refsDeleted includes barrier/issue-13/n1; issue-50 state file is a DIRECTORY (EISDIR) →
barrier/issue-50/n1 deleted. Mechanism: parseStateFile's only throw is fs.readFileSync (active-folders.js:196),
caught per-folder with continue (:246), so present-but-unreadable evidence → tag silently dropped. Directory-
granularity abort (chmod-000 on kaola-workflow/ → readdirSync throw → aborted:true, repoD both refs survived)
does NOT extend to file granularity. Sequence runs have NO running-set.json → signal (a) is the sole keeper, no
redundancy.

## Surface 2 — tag vs sanitize(folder): CLOSED
Ref tag = sanitizeBarrierTag(basename(dirname(planPath))) (plan-validator.js:2911) = the folder basename, same
input the keep set sanitizes (shared sanitizeBarrierTag, claim.js:1934). Bundle bundle-683-684-685 kept (repoA);
real repo: barrier/issue-686/{n1,n2,n3} tag == folder; 16 historical bundle tags == folder names. Case mismatch
covered by keepLower (repoC folder Issue-20 / tag issue-20 → kept).

## Surface 3 — running-set walk path: CLOSED
Walk <scanRoot>/kaola-workflow/*/.cache/running-set.json; writers place it at dirname(planPath)/.cache. Real repo:
listBarrierSweepWorktreeRoots(mainRoot) = [main, .kw/worktrees/issue-686]; live folder under BOTH roots (dual
signal-a); keep sim kept exactly issue-686 (3 refs), 43 archived tags WOULD DELETE. Legs enumerated for free.

## Surface 4 — archive-reap collateral: CLOSED
Real archiveProjectDir (repoB): archiving issue-68 deleted only barrier/issue-68/{n1,n2}; issue-686/* and
leg-base/issue-68/n1 survived; bundle-683 left bundle-683-684/* intact (path-component-bounded for-each-ref).
Two concurrent archives reaped exactly their own tags, zero cross-talk, fail-soft intact.

## Surface 5 — deletion refname edges + TOCTOU: CLOSED
Multi-slash node components kept/deleted correctly; naked tag-level ref (no node) skipped (indexOf('/')<0);
refnames cannot carry LF/space (git-check-ref-format); tags [A-Za-z0-9_-] only. Forced enum failure → aborted;
non-repo cwd → aborted. TOCTOU: keep-build precedes enumeration in one 81ms process; ref creation trails
folder+state by the multi-process claim→freeze→open pipeline (seconds); a ref visible at enumerate implies its
folder existed at scan; the reverse race resolves to KEEP (under-reap).

## Verdict
REFUTED (high). One executed third-class over-reap: R8 (present-but-unreadable state file → live gc-anchor
reaped), contradicting the code's own "cannot prove dead ⇒ keep" discipline at file granularity. Universe
(all-worktrees), parse (NUL-exact), tag derivation, running-set path, archive-reap scoping, deletion, TOCTOU all
attacked and held. Fix is tighten-only + sweep-local. No repo file modified; real repo ref count unchanged (258).

receipts.body:
evidence-binding: n2-review 0d5689b3e4c9
verdict: pass
findings_blocking: 0
upstream_read: n1-reap-sweep e725d95472fb

finding: id=R8 scope=in_scope action=fix status=resolved severity=high fix_role=none rationale=present-but-unreadable workflow-state.md (EACCES chmod-000 + EISDIR) now KEPT via sweep-local keep-pass (c) at claim.js:3264-3277; cross-root repro executed (both live anchors survived), mutation-kill proven (neutralize keep.add → exactly the 4 R8 assertions RED, 241 passed), not keep-everything (dead-no-folder + empty-folder still reaped), sweep-local (shared active-folders.js untouched), parity ×4
finding: id=R9 scope=in_scope action=fix status=resolved severity=low fix_role=none rationale=#690 discharged in-run — nlPath686g hoisted above the R6 try, finally removes the sibling worktree dir (guarded by `if (nlPath686g)`); executed: today's runs leave tmpdir clean, stale pre-fix leak removed; close #690
finding: id=R10 scope=pre_existing action=follow_up status=deferred filed=#691 severity=low fix_role=none rationale=chmod-000 project DIRECTORY variant still reaped (existsSync returns false on EACCES-through-parent, so keep-pass (c) skips it); pre-dates the R8 delta, narrower + more exotic (self-inflicted chmod-000 on a live project dir, no shipped flow produces it); filed #691 with the exact statSync-ENOENT-vs-fault fix; narrows not widens under R8

# n2-review re-review (attempt 4) — R8 fix + #690 discharge

## 1. R8 closed (executed, cross-root)
Fresh scratch repo, live projects in a LINKED worktree, sweep from the MAIN root: (a) EACCES chmod-000 state file,
no running-set.json → tagsKept includes issue-eacceslive, ref survived; (b) EISDIR (state file is a directory) →
issue-eisdirlive kept. In-suite R8a (root-skip guarded via getuid) + R8b green in 245. Fix at claim.js:3264-3277:
existsSync distinguishes present-vs-absent, readFileSync throw → keep.add; success adds nothing.

## 2. Not keep-everything (executed)
Same single sweep: issue-deadnofolder (no folder) REAPED; issue-emptyfolder (dir, no state file) REAPED
(existsSync false → continue, no false keep). R8a/R8b each also assert their co-located dead tag reaped.

## 3. Sweep-local (verified)
git diff = exactly the 5 declared files; shared active-folders.js UNTOUCHED in every edition. Pass (c) reuses the
same `entries` listing as pass (b) (no extra readdirSync), identical entry guards, only effect is keep.add
(additive-only); a readFileSync throw is the catch body → cannot trip the outer whole-sweep abort.

## 4. Mutation-kill (executed)
Neutralize the pass (c) keep.add (line 3274) → RED with EXACTLY the 4 R8 assertions failing (241 passed), mutated
sweep reaped both live anchors. Restore cmp byte-identical → 245 green; canonical↔codex cmp identical.

## 5. #690 discharged (executed)
`let nlPath686g;` hoisted at test-claim-hardening.js:2663 above the try (2664); finally (2705-2708) references it
guarded by `if (nlPath686g)` — in scope, no ReferenceError. tmpdir forensics: one STALE pre-fix leak
(mtime 2026-07-14T21:00Z) existed; today's runs created zero new; removed the stale, re-ran → 245 green, tmpdir
clean. Leak closed → close #690.

## 6. No regression (executed)
245 assertions green (run 4×). All prior markers present: R1 ×4, R4 ×9, R5 ×3, R6 ×5, R7 ×5, enum-fail seam,
archive reap ×10, fail-soft ×4, collision ×11, running-set keep ×14, leg-base ×6.

## 7. Edition parity (executed)
edition-sync.js --check green (10 ports, 24 mirrors, 27 byte-identical groups); validate-script-sync OK; keep-pass
(c) `#686 R8` present ×2 in all 4 copies; canonical↔codex cmp byte-identical.

## Residual (R10, non-blocking, filed #691)
A chmod-000 project DIRECTORY (unprobeable through the unsearchable parent) is still reaped — executed
(tagsDeleted=["issue-dirlocked"]). existsSync returns false on EACCES-through-parent, so keep-pass (c) skips it.
NOT the R8 finding (both R8 shapes closed + mutation-killed); pre-dates the delta; narrows under it. Fix
(statSync ENOENT-vs-fault distinction) filed #691.

APPROVE — R8 closed (executed, mutation-killed, not keep-all), #690 discharged (executed), zero regressions across
245, parity ×4. Change gate passes.

# Adversarial gate — #686 reap/sweep FINAL pass (real-flow reachability)

## Surface 1 — lifecycle states with no keep signal: CLOSED (real CLI drives)
Real startup/claim/--record-base drives: P0 post-claim pre-plan (folder+state, no refs) — nothing to reap. P1
post-record-base SEQUENCE run (NO running-set.json), swept from main AND worktree root → refs KEPT (signal (a)
alone). Status matrix stamped in BOTH copies (active/in_progress/planning/paused/halted/escalated/consent_halt/
unknown/Active/custom/empty) → ALL kept; readActiveFolders excludes only released/closed/abandoned and the only
status writers are writeState ('active') + stampTerminalState (closed/abandoned, INSIDE archiveProjectDir) —
no live-reachable status is excluded. 'released' has NO writer (cmdRelease archives in the same call). Empty
state file → unknown → KEPT. Crash-window (closed in main copy, active in worktree) → KEPT. Real bundle
(startup --target-issues 201,202): folder==bundle_id==tag==bundle-201-202, kept. Real keep-open finalize:
only barrier/issue-102/* reaped, sibling byte-identical. Real repair cycle (--drop-base→sweep→--record-base→sweep):
kept at every interleave. record-base cannot precede state (guard prologue refuses without workflow-state.md).

## Surface 2 — concurrency without sabotage: CLOSED
Worktree-cwd claim (R4 class) kept from main + foreign worktree; leg-root claim kept. Hammer: 212 sweeps from
2 roots racing 6 real claims + record-bases + 1 concurrent discard → zero live-ref losses, FAIL=0. TOCTOU: whole
sweep = 0.08s wall (interior gap smaller) vs 0.218s state-write→ref-create floor (real runs: minutes) → a ref
old enough to enumerate has a state file ≥218ms old, visible to the scan that precedes the enum by <80ms.
Ordering-closed. Worktree ADDED mid-sweep carries only a ref ≥218ms out (unseen); leg REMOVED takes its folder.

## Surface 3 — archive-time reap boundary: CLOSED (+ R11)
Real discard (release --project issue-1) with live issue-10/issue-101 → only barrier/issue-1/* reaped,
component-boundary confirmed. Real keep-open finalize → exact-tag reap only. NEW edge (R11, out-of-scope):
claim --project "issue.500" + "issue_500" both anchor barrier/issue_500/n1 (second record-base clobbers the first
— pre-existing ref-keying alias yielding barrier_base_mismatch upstream); release --project issue.500 then deleted
the live sibling anchor. Requires hand-crafted aliasing names through bare cmdClaim; every shipped surface emits
issue-N/bundle-N-M (sanitize fixed-points, cannot alias); upstream-broken. Sweep side collision-safe (dangling
barrier/issue_500/n9 KEPT while folder issue_500 lives).

## Surface 4 — stale/missing keep signals: CLOSED
Archived project's running-set.json travels into kaola-workflow/archive/ (whole-folder move) and the sweep skips
`archive` → no stale keep for dead tags. Live sequence run without running-set.json protected by (a) alone
(Surface 1 P1). Fail-closed: FORCE_BARRIER_WT_LIST_FAIL=1 with a dead dangling tag → aborted:true, zero deletions;
next normal sweep reaped exactly the dead issue-999, kept all live, left leg-base/* untouched.

## What I could NOT find
No reachable lifecycle point where a live barrier ref exists while (a),(b),(c) all fail on every root: every
shipped flow writes status:active before any ref can exist, never rewrites to terminal outside archiveProjectDir,
never deletes workflow-state.md, truncate-writes parse to a kept status. No timing window fits the ordering
(0.08s vs 0.218s; 212-sweep hammer clean). No archive-reap cross-tag bleed with shipped names. Remaining
over-reaps are exactly the accepted boundary (R8 fixed, R10 #691) plus R11 (hand-crafted names, upstream-broken).
Suite: 245 assertions pass.

## Verdict
NOT-REFUTED (high). Surfaces 1-4 driven with real CLI flows in real git scratch repos; every reachable live state
keeps its refs; only accepted out-of-band-FS-damage edges (R8 fixed, R10 #691) + the out-of-scope hand-crafted-name
aliasing edge (R11, documented) remain. No repo file modified.
